threats.title: XXE via XML upload with noent:true — routes/fileUpload.ts
threats.description: The XML upload handler uses libxmljs2 with `noent: true`, which enables external entity processing. An attacker can upload an XML file with an XXE payload pointing to file:///etc/passwd or internal network URLs, causing the server to disclose local file contents or SSRF to internal services.

evidence.excerpt: const xmlDoc = vm.runInContext('libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: true, nocdata: true })', sandbox, { timeout: 2000 })

mitigation.title: Disable external entity processing in XML parser
steps: Change `noent: true` to `noent: false` in libxmljs2 options
Validate XML against a schema before processing
Consider rejecting XML uploads entirely if the B2B complaint use case can be deprecated

threats.title: Directory traversal in ZIP file upload — routes/fileUpload.ts
threats.description: The ZIP file upload handler extracts entries to uploads/complaints/ without sufficient path validation. The `absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.'))` check can be bypassed with carefully crafted paths. An attacker can write files to arbitrary locations including ftp/ (e.g. ftp/legal.md), enabling overwrite of sensitive server files.

evidence.excerpt:
const absolutePath = path.resolve('uploads/complaints/' + fileName)
  challengeUtils.solveIf(challenges.fileWriteChallenge, () => { return absolutePath === path.resolve('ftp/legal.md') })

mitigation.title: Use path.resolve with strict prefix validation for ZIP extraction
steps: After resolving the path, verify it starts with `path.resolve('uploads/complaints') + path.sep`
Use the archiver library's safeExtract option or manually validate each entry
Reject ZIP entries with absolute paths or path traversal sequences

threats.title: SSRF via profile image URL upload — routes/profileImageUrlUpload.ts
threats.description: The profile image URL upload endpoint fetches images from user-supplied URLs server-side without validation of the URL scheme or host. An attacker can supply internal URLs (file://, http://169.254.169.254/metadata, http://localhost:3000/metrics) to probe internal services or read local files via the SSRF vector.

evidence.excerpt: Fetches profile image from user-supplied URL without scheme/host validation

mitigation.title: Implement SSRF prevention for URL-based image upload
steps: Validate URL scheme is http:// or https:// only
Implement allowlist of permitted image hosts
Use DNS resolution check to block requests to private IP ranges (RFC 1918)
Consider using a dedicated image proxy service

threats.title: Denial of Service via XML billion-laughs attack — routes/fileUpload.ts
threats.description: The XML parser with `noent: true` is vulnerable to billion-laughs (XML DoS) attacks where entity expansion causes exponential memory allocation. While vm.runInContext has a 2000ms timeout, memory exhaustion can still crash the Node.js process before the timeout triggers.

evidence.excerpt: libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: true, nocdata: true })

threats.controls_in_place: 2000ms execution timeout (does not prevent memory exhaustion)

mitigation.title: Disable entity expansion and add file size limit
steps: Disable `noent: true` to prevent entity expansion
Enforce maximum XML document size (e.g., 100KB) before parsing
Consider rate-limiting the file upload endpoint
